items.title: EPSS Surge: CVE-2012-0158
items.analysis: EPSS exploitation probability for CVE-2012-0158 surged from 0% to 100% (percentile 100%), indicating a significant increase in predicted or observed exploitation activity for this 2012-era vulnerability. It is not (yet) in CISA KEV — this signal typically precedes KEV listing and press coverage. The (1) ListView, (2) ListView2, (3) TreeView, and (4) TreeView2 ActiveX controls in MSCOMCTL.OCX in the Common Controls in Microsoft Office 2003 SP3, 2007 SP2 and SP3, and 2010 Gold and SP1; Office 2003 Web Components SP3; SQL Server 2000 SP4, 2005 SP4, and 2008 SP2, SP3, and R2; BizTalk Server 2002 SP1; Commerce Server 2002 SP4, 2007 SP2, and 2009 Gold and R2; Visual FoxPro 8.0 SP1 and 9.0 SP2; and Visual Basic 6.0 Runtime allow remote attackers to execute arbitrary code via a crafted (a) web site, (b) Office document, or (c) .rtf file that triggers "system state" corruption, as exploited in the wild in April 2012, aka "MSCOMCTL.OCX RCE Vulnerability."
items.description: The (1) ListView, (2) ListView2, (3) TreeView, and (4) TreeView2 ActiveX controls in MSCOMCTL.OCX in the Common Controls in Microsoft Office 2003 SP3, 2007 SP2 and SP3, and 2010 Gold and SP1; Office 2003 Web Components SP3; SQL Server 2000 SP4, 2005 SP4, and 2008 SP2, SP3, and R2; BizTalk Server 2002 SP1; Commerce Server 2002 SP4, 2007 SP2, and 2009 Gold and R2; Visual FoxPro 8.0 SP1 and 9.0 SP2; and Visual Basic 6.0 Runtime allow remote attackers to execute arbitrary code via a crafted (a) web site, (b) Office document, or (c) .rtf file that triggers "system state" corruption, as exploited in the wild in April 2012, aka "MSCOMCTL.OCX RCE Vulnerability."

items.title: EPSS Surge: CVE-2012-1823
items.analysis: EPSS exploitation probability for CVE-2012-1823 surged from 0% to 100% (percentile 100%), indicating a significant increase in predicted or observed exploitation activity for this 2012-era vulnerability. It is not (yet) in CISA KEV — this signal typically precedes KEV listing and press coverage. sapi/cgi/cgi_main.c in PHP before 5.3.12 and 5.4.x before 5.4.2, when configured as a CGI script (aka php-cgi), does not properly handle query strings that lack an = (equals sign) character, which allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code by placing command-line options in the query string, related to lack of skipping a certain php_getopt for the 'd' case.
items.description: sapi/cgi/cgi_main.c in PHP before 5.3.12 and 5.4.x before 5.4.2, when configured as a CGI script (aka php-cgi), does not properly handle query strings that lack an = (equals sign) character, which allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code by placing command-line options in the query string, related to lack of skipping a certain php_getopt for the 'd' case.

items.title: EPSS Surge: CVE-2013-2251 (Apache HTTP Server)
items.analysis: EPSS exploitation probability for CVE-2013-2251 surged from 0% to 100% (percentile 100%), indicating a significant increase in predicted or observed exploitation activity for this 2013-era vulnerability. It is not (yet) in CISA KEV — this signal typically precedes KEV listing and press coverage. Apache Struts 2.0.0 through 2.3.15 allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary OGNL expressions via a parameter with a crafted (1) action:, (2) redirect:, or (3) redirectAction: prefix.

items.title: EPSS Surge: CVE-2014-0160
items.analysis: EPSS exploitation probability for CVE-2014-0160 surged from 0% to 100% (percentile 100%), indicating a significant increase in predicted or observed exploitation activity for this 2014-era vulnerability. It is not (yet) in CISA KEV — this signal typically precedes KEV listing and press coverage. The (1) TLS and (2) DTLS implementations in OpenSSL 1.0.1 before 1.0.1g do not properly handle Heartbeat Extension packets, which allows remote attackers to obtain sensitive information from process memory via crafted packets that trigger a buffer over-read, as demonstrated by reading private keys, related to d1_both.c and t1_lib.c, aka the Heartbleed bug.
items.description: The (1) TLS and (2) DTLS implementations in OpenSSL 1.0.1 before 1.0.1g do not properly handle Heartbeat Extension packets, which allows remote attackers to obtain sensitive information from process memory via crafted packets that trigger a buffer over-read, as demonstrated by reading private keys, related to d1_both.c and t1_lib.c, aka the Heartbleed bug.

items.title: EPSS Surge: CVE-2014-0195
items.analysis: EPSS exploitation probability for CVE-2014-0195 surged from 0% to 100% (percentile 100%), indicating a significant increase in predicted or observed exploitation activity for this 2014-era vulnerability. It is not (yet) in CISA KEV — this signal typically precedes KEV listing and press coverage. The dtls1_reassemble_fragment function in d1_both.c in OpenSSL before 0.9.8za, 1.0.0 before 1.0.0m, and 1.0.1 before 1.0.1h does not properly validate fragment lengths in DTLS ClientHello messages, which allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code or cause a denial of service (buffer overflow and application crash) via a long non-initial fragment.
items.description: The dtls1_reassemble_fragment function in d1_both.c in OpenSSL before 0.9.8za, 1.0.0 before 1.0.0m, and 1.0.1 before 1.0.1h does not properly validate fragment lengths in DTLS ClientHello messages, which allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code or cause a denial of service (buffer overflow and application crash) via a long non-initial fragment.

items.title: EPSS Surge: CVE-2014-3566
items.analysis: EPSS exploitation probability for CVE-2014-3566 surged from 0% to 100% (percentile 100%), indicating a significant increase in predicted or observed exploitation activity for this 2014-era vulnerability. It is not (yet) in CISA KEV — this signal typically precedes KEV listing and press coverage. The SSL protocol 3.0, as used in OpenSSL through 1.0.1i and other products, uses nondeterministic CBC padding, which makes it easier for man-in-the-middle attackers to obtain cleartext data via a padding-oracle attack, aka the "POODLE" issue.
items.description: The SSL protocol 3.0, as used in OpenSSL through 1.0.1i and other products, uses nondeterministic CBC padding, which makes it easier for man-in-the-middle attackers to obtain cleartext data via a padding-oracle attack, aka the "POODLE" issue.

items.title: EPSS Surge: CVE-2014-3704
items.analysis: EPSS exploitation probability for CVE-2014-3704 surged from 0% to 100% (percentile 100%), indicating a significant increase in predicted or observed exploitation activity for this 2014-era vulnerability. It is not (yet) in CISA KEV — this signal typically precedes KEV listing and press coverage. The expandArguments function in the database abstraction API in Drupal core 7.x before 7.32 does not properly construct prepared statements, which allows remote attackers to conduct SQL injection attacks via an array containing crafted keys.
items.description: The expandArguments function in the database abstraction API in Drupal core 7.x before 7.32 does not properly construct prepared statements, which allows remote attackers to conduct SQL injection attacks via an array containing crafted keys.

items.title: EPSS Surge: CVE-2014-6271 (Apache HTTP Server)
items.analysis: EPSS exploitation probability for CVE-2014-6271 surged from 0% to 100% (percentile 100%), indicating a significant increase in predicted or observed exploitation activity for this 2014-era vulnerability. It is not (yet) in CISA KEV — this signal typically precedes KEV listing and press coverage. GNU Bash through 4.3 processes trailing strings after function definitions in the values of environment variables, which allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code via a crafted environment, as demonstrated by vectors involving the ForceCommand feature in OpenSSH sshd, the mod_cgi and mod_cgid modules in the Apache HTTP Server, scripts executed by unspecified DHCP clients, and other situations in which setting the environment occurs across a privilege boundary from Bash execution, aka "ShellShock."  NOTE: the original fix for this issue was incorrect; CVE-2014-7169 has been assigned to cover the vulnerability that is still present after the incorrect fix.
items.description: GNU Bash through 4.3 processes trailing strings after function definitions in the values of environment variables, which allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code via a crafted environment, as demonstrated by vectors involving the ForceCommand feature in OpenSSH sshd, the mod_cgi and mod_cgid modules in the Apache HTTP Server, scripts executed by unspecified DHCP clients, and other situations in which setting the environment occurs across a privilege boundary from Bash execution, aka "ShellShock."  NOTE: the original fix for this issue was incorrect; CVE-2014-7169 has been assigned to cover the vulnerability that is still present after the incorrect fix.

items.title: EPSS Surge: CVE-2015-1635 (Microsoft Windows)
items.analysis: EPSS exploitation probability for CVE-2015-1635 surged from 0% to 100% (percentile 100%), indicating a significant increase in predicted or observed exploitation activity for this 2015-era vulnerability. It is not (yet) in CISA KEV — this signal typically precedes KEV listing and press coverage. HTTP.sys in Microsoft Windows 7 SP1, Windows Server 2008 R2 SP1, Windows 8, Windows 8.1, and Windows Server 2012 Gold and R2 allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code via crafted HTTP requests, aka "HTTP.sys Remote Code Execution Vulnerability."
items.description: HTTP.sys in Microsoft Windows 7 SP1, Windows Server 2008 R2 SP1, Windows 8, Windows 8.1, and Windows Server 2012 Gold and R2 allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code via crafted HTTP requests, aka "HTTP.sys Remote Code Execution Vulnerability."
